symbols.name: SpinEventEmitter
symbols.detail: Auto-promoted: exported class imported by 1 file — abstraction built for a single client

symbols.name: SPIN_DONE
symbols.detail: [USED] Exported constant with 1 runtime importer in src/engine.ts | [UNIQUE] No RAG data available | [OK] Exported string constant; no correctness issues. | [LEAN] Single exported string constant. ADR-004 explicitly documents its purpose: prevent string-literal typos in consumer code. Minimal and appropriate.

file_level.general_notes: Best practices score: 7.5/10 (0 FAIL, 5 WARN, 12 PASS)

rules.detail: tsconfig.json is not visible, but the consistent use of `unknown` over `any` is strongly indicative of strict mode being enabled project-wide. No evidence of violations within this file.
`unknown` is used throughout — in `EventHandler`, `emit`, and the rest-parameter spread — with no `any` present.
No union types are defined or needed in this file. Not applicable.
`Map<string, EventHandler[]>` is the appropriate data structure here. No utility types (Pick, Omit, etc.) are needed.

Two issues: (1) `private listeners` should be `private readonly listeners` — the Map reference is never reassigned. (2) `SPIN_DONE = "spin:done"` widens to `string`; adding `as const` narrows it to the literal type `"spin:done"`, which prevents mismatched string comparisons at call-sites.
`type EventHandler` is the correct choice for a function-signature alias. Consistent with idiomatic TypeScript conventions.
No obvious lint violations. Nullish coalescing, `for...of`, and `filter` usage are all idiomatic and lint-clean.

Neither the `SpinEventEmitter` class nor its public methods (`on`, `off`, `emit`) carry JSDoc. `SPIN_DONE` is also undocumented. ADR-004 in `.anatoly/docs/02-Architecture/04-Design-Decisions.md` describes the lifecycle contract — that intent should be surfaced as JSDoc for API consumers.
Uses `Map`, nullish coalescing (`??`), `for...of`, and `unknown` — all idiomatic modern TypeScript. No deprecated APIs.
No imports in this file. Zero-dependency by design per ADR-004 (`.anatoly/docs/02-Architecture/04-Design-Decisions.md`). File placement in `src/` is consistent with the documented project structure.

The `emit` loop has no try-catch around handler invocations. If any single handler throws (e.g. a logging subscriber), all subsequent handlers are silently skipped. Per ADR-004, handlers include logging, analytics, AND free-spin chaining — a throw from logging would prevent the free-spin chain from firing, a real business-logic hazard in this casino domain. Consider wrapping each `handler(...args)` call in a try-catch.

No hardcoded secrets, no `eval`, no command injection. This file is a pure structural utility (pub-sub bus) with no RNG or financial arithmetic, so gambling-domain amplification rules (Math.random, floating-point money) do not apply here.
`off` creates a filtered array copy — appropriate for correctness and not a concern at typical event-emitter call frequencies. No sync I/O or N+1 patterns.
Zero external dependencies, no global state, all behaviour is driven through the public API. Highly testable in isolation.

`SPIN_DONE` is a good candidate for `as const` (narrowing to literal type). More impactfully, a typed event-map approach using `satisfies` or a const type parameter on `SpinEventEmitter` would eliminate the `unknown` cast burden on consumers while remaining TS 5.5+ idiomatic.

As a utility event bus, the current `(...args: unknown[]) => void` signature forces every consumer to unsafely cast the event payload (e.g., `result as SpinResult`). For a pub-sub emitter in a casino engine, a typed event map (generic over an event→payload record) would preserve type safety end-to-end and is the idiomatic pattern for TypeScript utility libraries.

suggestions.description: Narrow `SPIN_DONE` to its literal type and mark `listeners` as readonly
Guard `emit` so a throwing handler cannot abort delivery to subsequent handlers
Introduce a typed event map to eliminate `unknown` casts at call-sites
suggestions.before:
type EventHandler = (...args: unknown[]) => void;
export class SpinEventEmitter { ... }

suggestions.description: Add JSDoc to exported symbols to document the lifecycle contract
